experiment.name: step-3 implementation-shape
experiment.purpose: Translate the selected Cursor Mirror prediction model into an O(1), allocation-free product implementation shape.
experiment.generated_by: manual Step 3 implementation-shape review plus run_microbenchmark.ps1

selected_model.name: constant-velocity-last2
selected_model.reason: Step 1 selected constant-velocity-last2 with no cap for every nonzero tested horizon on the latter 30% split. Step 2 found damping improves some longer-horizon means but does not consistently improve p95/p99/max, and online expert selection worsened tail behavior.

add_sample_first_or_invalid.notes: Stores current sample and clears velocity when history is unavailable or interval is invalid.

optional_cap_extra_cost.extra_operations: offset length squared, comparison, optional sqrt and rescale
optional_cap_extra_cost.reason_disabled: Step 1 fixed caps worsened mean and tail metrics by clipping legitimate fast movement.

memory_allocation_expectations.estimated_state_bytes: approximately 64-80 bytes depending on runtime layout

advanced_horizon_dependent_gain.warning: Mean improves at longer horizons but p95/p99/max do not consistently improve.

risk_flags.description: Model ranking is based on one captured trace. More users/devices/display setups may shift results.
Prediction remains visibly risky at 24ms and above; default horizon should stay in the 8-16ms band.
Fixed offset caps worsened Step 1 accuracy; any cap should be disabled by default or treated as a high failsafe.
Mixing hook timestamps, Environment.TickCount, and wall-clock time can produce invalid intervals.
Prediction must not affect click targets, but visual overshoot around button transitions may require exact-position sync.

measured_timing.measurement_type: single local noisy synthetic .NET update-plus-predict loop
measured_timing.interpretation: Sanity check only. The measured loop is far below a typical UI frame budget, but production timing should be measured after integration.

implementation_readiness.unit_testability: Can be tested with synthetic timestamp and position streams; no real Windows hook required.
implementation_readiness.product_recommendation: Implement behind a setting or feature flag, default to last2/gain 1/no cap, and keep damping/caps experimental.
